mnemonics.title: Wind Speed Control Points
mnemonics.content: Remember as: 'CUT' - Cut-in, Under control (rated), Trouble (cut-out). Cut-in starts power, Rated gives max, Cut-out stops for safety.

mnemonics.title: Turbine Components
mnemonics.content: Remember as: 'BONG' - Blade, Operating system (gearbox/generator), Nacelle, Generator. Actually remember: 'TANGO' - Tower, Axis, Nacelle, Gearbox, Output (generator).

acronyms.usage: Measures wind energy potential per unit area in W/m², used for site assessment.
Most common commercial turbine type with horizontal rotating shaft.
Turbines with vertical rotating shaft, suitable for urban and low-wind areas.
Ratio of blade tip speed to wind speed, critical for turbine design optimization.
Total cost of building and operating a power plant over its lifetime divided by total energy output.
Sets international standards for wind turbine design and testing.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a cube curve: x-axis = wind speed, y-axis = power. Show power increases with cube of speed. Remember: small speed increase = big power jump.
Imagine a funnel: wind enters wide, exits narrow. Maximum 59.3% energy extraction. Draw turbine as partial blocker - air must still pass through.
Three zones on speed axis: Zone 1 (below cut-in) = no power, Zone 2 (cut-in to rated) = power increases with v³, Zone 3 (above rated) = constant power controlled, Zone 4 (above cut-out) = shutdown.

analogies.analogy: Like an airplane wing - air moves faster on top, creating lift that makes blades spin. Aerofoil shape is crucial for efficiency.
Like rainfall catchment - bigger bucket (larger area) catches more rain, and heavier rain (faster wind = denser 'rain') catches much more.
Like a ceiling fan's safety switch - if too fast (overload), it automatically turns off to prevent damage.
Like a car that only runs 8 hours a day at full speed - capacity factor is actual output vs maximum possible if running 24/7.
